title: Pouteria boniana

Pouteria boniana is a member of the genus Pouteria, which consists of trees and shrubs. The Sapotaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as sapotes or soapberries. Pouteria boniana is often found growing in tropical forests and woodlands.

intro: Pouteria boniana, also known as Vietnamese camphor or camphor tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Vietnamese medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria boniana include camphor, borneol, bornyl acetate, and α-pinene, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pouteria boniana is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
